mapper.sql: id, username, password, nickname, email, phone, dept_id, status, auth_version, create_by, create_time, update_by, update_time, remark, del_flag
mapper.select:
SELECT user_row.id, user_row.username, user_row.nickname, user_row.email, user_row.phone, user_row.dept_id, user_row.status, user_row.create_time FROM ( SELECT u.id FROM sys_user u
ORDER BY u.create_time DESC, u.id DESC LIMIT #{offset}, #{pageSize} ) page_ids INNER JOIN sys_user user_row ON user_row.id = page_ids.id ORDER BY user_row.create_time DESC, user_row.id DESC
mapper.update:
UPDATE sys_user SET auth_version = auth_version + 1 WHERE id IN
#{userId}
UPDATE sys_authorization_state SET global_auth_version = global_auth_version + 1 WHERE id = 1
mapper.select: SELECT u.id, u.status, u.auth_version, u.del_flag, state.global_auth_version FROM sys_user u INNER JOIN sys_authorization_state state ON state.id = 1 WHERE u.id = #{userId} LIMIT 1

SELECT u.id, u.username, u.password, u.nickname, u.email, u.phone, u.dept_id, u.status, u.auth_version, u.create_by, u.create_time, u.update_by, u.update_time, u.remark, u.del_flag, state.global_auth_version FROM sys_user u INNER JOIN sys_authorization_state state ON state.id = 1 WHERE u.id = #{userId} LIMIT 1

SELECT u.id, u.username, u.password, u.nickname, u.email, u.phone, u.dept_id, u.status, u.auth_version, u.create_by, u.create_time, u.update_by, u.update_time, u.remark, u.del_flag, state.global_auth_version FROM sys_user u INNER JOIN sys_authorization_state state ON state.id = 1 WHERE u.username = #{username} AND u.del_flag = 0 LIMIT 1
SELECT FROM sys_user WHERE id = #{userId} AND del_flag = 0 LIMIT 1 FOR UPDATE
